contentBlocks.original:
ॐ अपवित्रः पवित्रो वा सर्वावस्थां गतोऽपि वा ।
यः स्मरेत्पुण्डरीकाक्षं स बाह्याभ्यन्तरः शुचिः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ apavitraḥ pavitro vā sarvāvasthāṃ gato'pi vā .
yaḥ smaretpuṇḍarīkākṣaṃ sa bāhyābhyantaraḥ śuciḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: Om, if one is Apavitra (Impure) or Pavitra (Pure), or even in all other conditions, he who remembers Pundarikaksha (another name of Sri Vishnu, literally meaning with lotus-like eyes), he becomes pure outwardly as well as inwardly.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – Om; अपवित्रः (apavitraḥ) – impure; पवित्रः (pavitraḥ) – pure; वा (vā) – or; सर्वावस्थाम् (sarvāvasthām) – all conditions (sarva:all | avasthām:conditions); गतः (gataḥ) – gone; अपि (api) – even; वा (vā) – or; यः (yaḥ) – who; स्मरेत् (smaret) – remembers; पुण्डरीकाक्षम् (puṇḍarīkākṣam) – Pundarikaksha (puṇḍarīka:lotus | akṣam:eye); स (sa) – he; बाह्याभ्यन्तरः (bāhyābhyantaraḥ) – outwardly and inwardly (bāhya:outwardly | abhyantaraḥ:inwardly); शुचिः (śuciḥ) – pure;
